pattern: Row-by-row dynamic programming keeps the cheapest cost to reach each cell, combining its value with the best transition from the row above.
hints: You always descend exactly one row per move, so the cheapest way to reach a cell depends only on the cheapest ways to reach the cells in the previous row.
Carry a cost array for the current row; to fill the next row, each cell takes its own value plus the minimum over previous-row cells of their cost plus the move cost keyed by that cell's value.

Initialize the first row's costs to the grid values. For each subsequent row and each column j, set cost = grid value + min over previous columns p of (prevCost[p] + moveCost[gridValueOfPrevCell][j]). The answer is the minimum of the last row's costs.

edge_inputs.description: cheapest cell value but expensive move forces a tradeoff
uniform move costs so only cell values matter

stress.description: random grid that is a permutation of 0..m*n-1 with random positive move costs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randrange(2, 11)
    n = rng.randrange(2, 11)
    vals = list(range(m * n))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    grid = [vals[i * n:(i + 1) * n] for i in range(m)]
    moveCost = [[rng.randrange(1, 101) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m * n)]
    return [grid, moveCost]
